$comment: Quick-reference index of this ratchet's flows (id + summary + entry + node count). The engine discovers flows by scanning each flow folder's chain.json; this file is for fast human/agent reference. Update it when adding or changing a flow.

flows.summary: Plan-routed grounding, then generate Go for a focused task, verify it with `go build`, and repair once if it does not build.
Plan-routed grounding, then generate a Go implementation plus a test, verify it with `go vet` + `go test` (behavior, not just compile), and repair once if it fails.
Compose a Go module from a dir of specs: plan the build order, generate each unit in dependency order against the accumulated module, then build and test the whole thing.
Generate one composed Go unit against the accumulated module, build the whole module, repair once, register. (Called by compose's foreach.)
Add a new Go file to a workspace from a request; grounded on the module's existing API, verified with go vet + go test, repaired once, recorded.
Apply a change to an existing Go file in a workspace; grounded on its current contents, verified with go vet + go test, repaired once, logged.
Build and run a workspace's main, capturing stdout/stderr/exit (a server is stopped after a few seconds).
Answer a Go question in prose, grounded in the knowledge base (stdlib, Effective Go / Code Review Comments, patterns, pitfalls). A reference/learning path - no code generation.
Draft well-formed Go build spec(s) from a description (one unit or a whole system), validated and written into a workspace - the front-end to compose.
Run the full quality/security gate over a workspace (gofmt, vet, build, test -race, staticcheck, govulncheck, gosec); pass only if all present tools are clean.
Draft a kb/pitfalls entry for a recurring Go failure class (from mine_runs) and add it to the knowledge base - the self-improvement loop.
Type-safe rename across a module: the model maps a request to a target, gorename renames every reference and verifies the build.
Change a dependency/toolchain version in a workspace, verified with the full quality gate and rolled back if it breaks.
Apply one change across several files at once, verified atomically over the whole module (go vet + go test -race), with rollback - for cross-cutting edits.
Search a workspace's generated code by intent: rebuild the code index, BM25-rank the units against your query, return the best matches.
Apply one spec layer as a transactional multi-file edit: regenerate every added/changed unit together, verify the whole module once (go vet + go test -race), roll back on failure.
